🚀 GitHub Pages — Setup Complete

Jekyll-powered site with a modern dark theme deployed via GitHub Actions

⚡ 3-Step Deployment

01
Enable Pages
Repo → Settings → Pages → Source: GitHub Actions
02
Push to main
git add . && git commit -m "pages" && git push — workflow auto-builds & deploys
03
Update URL
Edit _config.yml: set url and baseurl to your GitHub Pages address

📁 Files Created

📁 devops-interview-guide/
├.github/workflows/
└pages.ymlNEW · CI deploy
├_layouts/
└default.htmlNEW · full page shell
├_includes/
└(reserved for partials)
├assets/css/
└style.cssNEW · 450 lines
├_config.ymlNEW · Jekyll config
├GemfileNEW · dependencies
├index.mdNEW · landing page
├README.mdUPDATED · deploy docs
└jd-openshift-k8s-engineer/
├01…09 modules + READMEexisting · auto-rendered

🎨 Design Features

🌑
Dark theme + gradient accents
Deep navy background with indigo/cyan/violet gradient headings and code blocks
📱
Fully responsive
Fixed topbar + collapsible sidebar on mobile, fluid content area on all screen sizes
📋
Copy button on every code block
Vanilla JS one-click copy with "copied!" feedback — no libraries needed
▾
Collapsible Q&A sections
Click any H3 question heading to collapse its answer — great for self-testing
🃏
Module cards with colour coding
Each module gets a unique accent colour with a coloured top bar and matching badge
🔍
Active sidebar + breadcrumbs
Current page highlighted in sidebar; breadcrumb trail at top of every page
🎨
Rouge syntax highlighting
GitHub Dark-style code highlighting for YAML, Python, Bash, Groovy, HCL, and more
⚡
Zero JS frameworks
Pure HTML + CSS + ~50 lines of vanilla JS — fast load, no bundler required

🎨 Colour Palette

🔧 Local Preview

cd devops-interview-guide
bundle install
bundle exec jekyll serve --livereload
# → http://localhost:4000
Made with IBM Bob
